title: AU: Civil Construction Induction
subtitle: Entry-Level Safety and Compliance — Australia
description: Essential knowledge for anyone starting in Australian civil construction. Covers the WHS Act, Safe Work Australia, HRW licensing, White Card, and Austroads standards.

lessons.title: Work Health and Safety Act 2011
lessons.content:
Australia uses a harmonised Work Health and Safety (WHS) system.

**PCBU — Person Conducting a Business or Undertaking**
The primary duty holder. Civil construction companies, subcontractors and sole traders are all PCBUs.

**Primary Duty of Care (Section 19)**
A PCBU must ensure, so far as is reasonably practicable:
- Health and safety of workers
- Health and safety of others affected by work

**Worker Duties (Section 28)**
- Take reasonable care for own health and safety
- Not adversely affect health and safety of others
- Comply with reasonable PCBU instructions

**State Regulators (Enforcement Bodies)**
NSW: SafeWork NSW | VIC: WorkSafe Victoria | QLD: WHSQ | WA: WorkSafe WA

lessons.title: Incident Reporting and Notifiable Events
lessons.content:
**Three Types of Notifiable Incidents (Must Report Immediately)**

1. Death of any person
2. Serious injury or illness — hospitalisation, amputation, serious burns, spinal injury, loss of consciousness
3. Dangerous incident — uncontrolled escape of hazardous substance, collapse of a structure, electric shock

**How to Report**
- Call your state regulator immediately — do not wait
- Follow up with written notification
- Preserve the scene — do not disturb without regulator permission

**Penalty for Non-Reporting**
Up to $50,000 for an individual, $250,000 for a corporation.

questions.q: What does PCBU stand for?
options: Person Conducting a Business or Undertaking

questions.q: Under the WHS Act, workers must:
options: Take reasonable care for their own and others health and safety
Report directly to Safe Work Australia

questions.q: A notifiable incident must be reported:
options: Immediately by phone to the state regulator
To Safe Work Australia within 24 hours

questions.q: Which body enforces WHS laws in New South Wales?

lessons.title: HRW Licensing — What Requires a Licence?
lessons.content:
**Key Fact: Excavators Do NOT Require an HRW Licence in Australia**
Operating earthmoving equipment does not require a government-issued licence. Employers must ensure operators are trained and competent.

**Activities That DO Require HRW Licence:**
- Scaffolding: Basic (SB), Intermediate (SI), Advanced (SA)
- Dogging and Rigging: DG, RB, RI, RA
- Cranes: Tower (CT), Vehicle Loading (CV), and more
- Elevated Work Platforms over 11m (WP)
- Forklifts (LF)

**HRW Licence Process:**
1. Complete approved training with a Registered Training Organisation (RTO)
2. Pass written and practical assessment
3. Apply to state regulator
4. Licence issued — valid nationally under mutual recognition

lessons.title: White Card — General Construction Induction
lessons.content:
**The White Card is Mandatory for ALL Construction Site Workers**

Legal Basis: Work Health and Safety Regulations 2017, Regulation 316

**What It Covers:**
- WHS legislation awareness
- Worker rights and responsibilities
- Hazard identification and risk management
- Incident reporting
- Emergency procedures

**Key Facts:**
- Valid nationally across all states
- No expiry date
- Must carry on site at all times
- Penalty: Up to $3,600 for working without White Card

**Difference from NZ:** New Zealand has no equivalent White Card requirement.

questions.q: Operating an excavator in Australia requires:
options: No HRW licence — but competency required

lessons.title: Austroads — The Road Standards Body
lessons.content:
**What is Austroads?**
Austroads is the peak body of Australasian road transport and traffic agencies. It develops technical guidelines used across Australia and New Zealand.

**Key Austroads Publications:**
- Guide to Pavement Technology (AGPT) — pavement design, materials, construction
- AGPT Part 4B — Bituminous binders
- AGPT Part 4D — Stabilised materials
- Guide to Road Design
- Guide to Traffic Management

**State Road Authorities:**
- NSW: Transport for NSW (TfNSW)
- VIC: VicRoads
- QLD: Department of Transport and Main Roads (TMR)
- WA: Main Roads WA

**Key Australian Standards:**
- AS 1742 series — Manual of Uniform Traffic Control Devices
- AS 2436 — Traffic management in work zones
- AS 1289 — Methods of testing soils

lessons.title: Traffic Management in Australia
lessons.content:
**Traffic Control on Australian Roads**
All roadwork requires a Traffic Management Plan (TMP) and qualified personnel.

**Core Training Units (National):**
- RIIWHS302E — Implement Traffic Management Plan
- RIIWHS205E — Control traffic with stop-slow bat

**State-Specific Requirements:**
- NSW: Traffic Control — Work Site Preparation
- VIC: Implement Traffic Management Plan
- QLD: RIIWHS302E required

**Key Rules:**
- TMP must be prepared by a qualified Traffic Management Designer
- All workers directing traffic must hold relevant state certification
- Minimum PPE: High-visibility vest, hard hat, safety footwear

**Call Before You Dig:** Dial 1100 before any excavation work in Australia.

questions.q: Austroads is best described as:
options: The peak body developing road transport technical guidelines for AU/NZ
An insurance body for road workers

questions.q: Which standard covers traffic management in Australian work zones?
Before excavating in Australia you must call:

lessons.title: PPE Requirements and Site Rules
lessons.content:
**Mandatory PPE on Australian Construction Sites:**
- Hard hat (AS/NZS 1801)
- High-visibility clothing — Class D/N day/night (AS/NZS 4602.1)
- Safety footwear — steel cap boots (AS/NZS 2210.3)
- Eye protection when required (AS/NZS 1337)
- Hearing protection in high-noise areas (AS/NZS 1269)

**Site Access Controls:**
- Sign in and out every day
- Attend site induction before starting work
- Follow Visitor/Worker identification requirements

**Working at Heights:**
Any work at 2m or above requires fall prevention measures:
- Guardrails (preferred)
- Safety mesh
- Fall arrest harness (last resort — requires training)

lessons.title: Hazardous Materials and Underground Services
lessons.content:
**Hazardous Materials in Civil Construction:**

**Asbestos**
- Found in older infrastructure built before 1990
- Stop work if asbestos suspected — contact supervisor
- Removal requires licensed asbestos removalist

**Silica Dust**
- Released when cutting/grinding concrete, rock, or sand
- Causes silicosis — serious and permanent lung disease
- Use wet cutting methods or P2 respirator

**Underground Services**
- Always call 1100 (Dial Before You Dig) before excavating
- Water, gas, electrical, telecommunications services all underground
- Use potholing to identify exact service locations
- Do not use machine within 300mm of a marked service

questions.q: Working at heights in Australia requires fall prevention at:
If you suspect asbestos on site you should:
options: Remove it yourself with a mask
Stop work and contact your supervisor
Cover it with soil and continue

questions.q: The preferred fall prevention method is:

lessons.title: Course Summary
lessons.content:
**Congratulations — You have completed AU: Civil Construction Induction!**

Key knowledge covered:

- WHS Act 2011: PCBU duties, reasonably practicable, worker duties
- Notifiable incidents: report immediately, preserve scene
- High Risk Work: 29 licence classes, excavators do NOT require HRW licence
- White Card: mandatory for all construction site workers, no expiry
- Austroads: road and pavement technical standards
- Traffic management: TMP required, state-specific certification
- Call 1100 before any excavation

**Key Differences vs NZ:**
- Australia uses PCBU (NZ uses employer/PCBU)
- White Card mandatory in AU (no equivalent in NZ)
- Austroads = NZTA technical equivalent
- 8 state regulators (NZ has one: WorkSafe NZ)
